name: Agora DC

notes: Every frozen row was reconciled against the current official Agora DC dinner, lunch, and brunch menus linked by the restaurant's menu hub.
Visual review of all six rendered PDF pages found 83 current products or distinct meal-period formulations across 18 real sections.

Ten frozen rows are headings, dietary-label fragments, add-on descriptions, or shifted neighboring descriptions; five older products are no longer current. The correction restores the omitted current menu.
Agora publishes item descriptions and GF, DF, and NF labels but no complete allergen matrix, complete recipes, or kitchen cross-contact policy.
Positive fields use fixed published ingredients and mandatory named formats. Negative dietary labels are not expanded into comprehensive allergen-free claims.
Twenty-one of 48 applicable frozen product rows mismatch: 11 underreported, six overreported, two mixed, and two collapsed meal-period formulations with different signals.
The general raw-food advisory is not applied to individual products; oyster mushrooms do not become shellfish and eggplant does not become egg.
The frozen PDF parser interleaved multi-column reading order, assigning neighboring descriptions, section labels, and raw-food warning words to unrelated items and fake categories.
The correction was transcribed and verified from rendered page layout, preserving section ownership and distinct brunch formulations where ingredients differ.
The repair is guarded by current counts, unique identities, artifact exclusions, meal-period variants, and sentinel allergen corrections.

sourceAttempts.outcome: Confirmed the DC identity and current official dinner, lunch, and brunch links.
Rendered and visually reviewed both current dinner pages with section, product, description, and dietary-label associations.
Rendered and visually reviewed both current lunch pages and all lunch-only products.
Rendered and visually reviewed the current brunch menu, including products whose formulations differ from dinner or lunch.
Reviewed all current food surfaces; found dietary labels and descriptions but no complete allergen matrix, recipes, or cross-contact policy.

findings.summary: The frozen 63-row catalog promoted ten headings or shifted fragments to products, retained five stale products, omitted current products, and assigned fake categories.
findings.resolution: Rebuilt 83 current products/formulations across 18 visually verified meal-period sections and removed all artifacts and stale rows.

findings.summary: Twenty-one applicable frozen rows disagree with their current rendered descriptions, including shellfish falsely assigned from oyster mushrooms or adjacent warning text.
findings.resolution: Recomputed fixed source-bounded signals per product/formulation and retained 15 current rows as unavailable where positive evidence is insufficient.

findings.summary: The frozen merge collapsed meal-period products and separated descriptions from their actual names, losing ingredient distinctions for same-name formulations.
findings.resolution: Preserved distinct Mixed Green Salad and Şiş Tavuk formulations where the current menus publish different ingredients.

repairs.summary: Prepared and focused-tested an 83-item/formulation Agora DC catalog across 18 current official sections with 68 positive rows, 15 unavailable rows, corrected artifacts, and meal-period-aware identities.
verificationCommands: RESTAURANT_REPOSITORY_PATH=/tmp/agora-repository.json node scripts/repair-final-generated-regressions.mjs (isolated copy; verified 83 items, 18 categories, 68 positive rows, artifacts excluded, variants retained, and sentinels corrected)

notes: Current corrected catalog counts: 83 products/formulations, 18 categories, 68 positive published-signal rows, and 15 unavailable rows.
The PDF skill's rendered-layout review determined section and description ownership; text extraction alone was rejected because it visibly interleaved columns.
